entries.title: <wave><grad #54F7FF #FFFFFF #B66DFF>Master Idle Rewards Added</grad></wave>
entries.summary: Selected masters can now gain passive friendship over time.

entries.title: Server Script Tab Optimization
entries.summary: Player scripts were reorganized to reduce tab clutter, avoid duplicate event handlers, and keep related systems grouped together.
details: Merged Daily/Weekly and EcoBridge into one unified economy and progression script.
Added a known-command registry so valid commands from other tabs no longer trigger Daily/Weekly unknown-action warnings.
Combined AFK, Wands, and Dragon Ball wish handling into one shared script for easier maintenance.
Moved form-vanity handling into the newer Tab 2 vanity sync system to prevent duplicate vanity/desync issues.
Unified HexSkills, Dragon Fist, Grapple skill commands, and Black Coffin skill commands into one skill-management script.
Updated Dragon Fist to use N-based combos while keeping Black Coffin on its synced mod keybind.
Added safe dispatcher routing for shared hooks like chat, timer, tick, attack, keyPressed, and GUI events.
The cleanup should make the server player scripts easier to manage while preserving existing functionality.
entries.actionSubtitle: Test merged tabs first, then remove old duplicate standalone tabs after confirming behavior.

entries.title: HexHair and DBCHairPlus Hair Fixes
entries.summary: HexHair and DBCHairPlus were updated so built-in DBC hair styles work with custom coloring, split colors, gradients, and animation support.
details: Added support for DBC built-in hair styles 1-12 instead of only supporting the custom/V2 hair render path.
Preserved the bald built-in hair slot so it should not be force-colored or treated as visible hair.
Applied the same built-in hair compatibility fix to DBCHairPlus so both hair systems stay aligned.
Built-in DBC hairs now support HexHair color behavior, including split colors, gradients, and animation support where applicable.
Protected Ultra Instinct and Mastered Ultra Instinct behavior so those forms should no longer be incorrectly repainted black or override their intended white hair behavior.
Kept the hair pass focused on actual hair render calls while avoiding face, eyes, mouth, tail, and other non-hair parts.

entries.title: Skills Balance and API Cleanup
entries.bannerTitle: Black Coffin and Dragon Fist tuning update
entries.summary: Recent skill work focused on Black Coffin PvP balance, Dragon Fist server stability, cleaner config pages, NBT unlock checks, and API documentation for scripting.
entries.actionSubtitle: PvP tuning is now mostly config-driven
details: Black Coffin damage tuning was moved further into server config so future PvP and PvE balance changes can be adjusted without rebuilding the mod.
Black Coffin now supports defense-aware PvP Body chip scaling, allowing target DBC Defense to reduce the guaranteed Body chip instead of ignoring defense entirely.
Black Coffin level checks were tightened: level 0 blocks charging and casting, level 1 allows normal only, level 2 allows chant, and level 3 unlocks the third-form modifier.
Black Coffin and Dragon Fist config options were reorganized under the unified Skills section, with shorter keys for cleaner config GUI display.
Legacy and duplicate config entries were cleaned up, including old Black Coffin, Dragon Fist, and build-height test options.
Dragon Fist server optimization was added to reduce lag from active Dragon Fist entities, including active-entity cleanup, tick throttling, and max lifetime safety checks.
Dragon Fist PvP damage was nerfed through config multipliers while keeping PvE damage mostly unchanged.
Custom PvP death messages were added for Dragon Fist and Black Coffin so player deaths no longer appear as unrelated mob kills.
Black Coffin PvP balance is now controlled mainly through bcPvpN, bcPvpC, bcPvpT, bcChipN, bcChipC, bcChipT, bcChipDefScale, and bcChipDefFloor.
Dragon Fist PvP balance is now controlled mainly through dfPvpN, dfPvpR, dfPvpL, dfPvpB, and dfPvpAll.
New API reference documents were prepared for HexMasterAPI, HexDragonFistAPI, and the broader HexColorText API index to make scripting support easier to maintain.
Memory cleanup and server diagnostic scripts were tested to help track script-side lag, item/entity buildup, and server memory usage during development.

entries.title: Version 1.5.8 Ready for CurseForge
entries.summary: Version 1.5.8 is ready to be uploaded to CurseForge, with major Master system polish, cached portraits, API additions, combat balance tweaks, and new scripting support.
details: Hex Master and Friendship system received its final polish pass for this update.
Master GUI now supports colorful friendship and completion bars, rank-colored lesson stars, TEST badges, COMPLETE stamps, and cleaner overall completion display.
Cached master portraits now stay rendered after walking away from loaded NPCs, using remembered/cached render data instead of falling back to initials during normal gameplay.
Portrait debug badges for live, remembered, dummy, and initials render states can now be hidden or shown through config.
Master API received new completion helpers for reading overall completion, completion bars, unlocked master counts, completed master counts, mastered master counts, and master ID lists.
New script support was prepared for master setup, master requirement tracking, quest/TP rank-up checks, overall completion milestone rewards, and transform blocking during special tests.
Rank-up requirements were tested with both quest checks and TP costs.
Black Coffin now has player-target damage multipliers so PvP damage can be tuned separately from NPC and mob damage.
Dragon Fist Ki costs were reduced to make the skill easier to use: Normal 12%, Rush 18%, Loop 22%, and Blast 19%.
Version 1.5.8 is now considered ready for a final build, PvP damage test, backup, and CurseForge upload.

entries.title: Masters and Dragon Fist Update
details: Added a script-supported Master's system with selectable NPC and player mentors.
Masters now use a Xenoverse-style progression loop where friendship fills per rank, then resets after a successful rank-up test.
Overall completion is tracked separately from friendship, allowing progress to continue across all four master ranks.
Added support for quest-based and TP-based rank-up tests, including fresh quest completion checks so old completed quests cannot instantly pass tests.
Updated the Master GUI with mini progress bars, gradient friendship/completion bars, TEST READY styling, colored rank stars, and player master previews.
Improved /hexmaster command permissions so players can use basic menu/status commands while admin-only subcommands remain OP-gated.
Improved command tab completion for existing master names and online player master setup.
Updated Dragon Fist script support with backup Ki checking and draining so Dragon Fist variants properly consume Ki when the mod-side drain path misses.

entries.title: <#ffbcb1>Dragon Fist & Grapple Skill Pass</#>
details: Dragon Fist now has script-activated key combos instead of a built-in mod keybind: K activates Dragon Fist, Shift + K activates Dragon Fist Rush, Ctrl + K activates Dragon Fist Loop, and Alt + K activates Dragon Fist Blast.
Dragon Fist skill checks now use PlayerPersisted.HexSkills instead of the older HitSkills example path. Each variant can be unlocked separately through NBT checks.

Dragon Fist API cooldown handling was moved mod-side so scripts cannot bypass cooldowns, Ki costs, or skill gates. Cooldowns are now enforced through the API and controller path instead of relying on player scripts.

Dragon Fist cooldowns were corrected to be per-variant instead of shared globally. Normal, Rush, Loop, and Blast now each use their own cooldown while still preventing multiple armed Rush/Loop/Blast windows from overwriting each other.
Dragon Fist now consumes heavy DBC Ki when used, making it behave more like an ultimate skill. The API path handles Ki consumption for player casts while NPC/script scaling remains flexible.
Normal Dragon Fist was reworked into a forward barrage/pass-through style attack. It now uses a narrow line hitbox so lined-up targets can be hit without turning the move into a large radial AOE.
Normal Dragon Fist carry and knockback were retuned so targets are dragged only slightly during the barrage instead of being launched too far across the map.
Rush Dragon Fist was adjusted to finish with an uppercut-style hit that launches the target upward without sending them too high.
Dragon Fist Rush and Blast now respect the enableDragonFistCommandDragonForm config option, so script activation no longer forces the player into dragon POV when the config is disabled.

Dragon Fist damage was moved back to mod-side balancing instead of script-side scaling. Base stat damage, method multipliers, and barrage hit multipliers were buffed so the skill feels stronger for high-melee builds.

The Dragon Fist player script was cleaned up with better hyperstyle-style messages, cooldown time display, per-variant titles, and invalid combo protection for mixed modifier inputs like Shift + Ctrl + K.

Grapple skill handling was also updated with HexSkills-based NBT checks, and its locked warning behavior was changed so the message is not repeatedly spammed every time a grapple keybind is pressed during the same login.

entries.title: <grad #FF7A00 #FFD36B #FFFFFF #FFD36B #FF7A00 scroll=0.18 styles=l>Dragon Fist Skill Expansion</grad>
entries.summary: Dragon Fist received scripting support, active-window skill behavior, stronger cinematic variants, and early DBC ki blast visual support.
details: Added script bridge support so CustomNPCs scripts can trigger Dragon Fist, Grapple, Black Coffin, and several orb-style effects without relying on command execution.
Dragon Fist can now be called from scripts with custom caster, target, method, base damage, and strength multiplier parameters.
Normal Dragon Fist is stable as the direct strike version and remains the clean fallback/debug style.
Rush Dragon Fist now uses an active melee window. The caster gains a visible ready effect and a small HUD indicator, then the next melee hit triggers the skill.
Rush now performs an opening strike, launches the target into a short arc, carries forward through the target, deals a barrage of hits, then vanishes.

Loop Dragon Fist now uses the same active-window style and was rebuilt into a GT-style cinematic variant: the dragon soars upward, loops through the air, dives back down, and only then begins the main damage barrage.
Loop damage and DBC Body handling were improved so scripted and NPC versions should hit DBC entities more reliably.
Dragon rendering was tuned with denser path history and more body segments for cinematic variants so the dragon appears more connected during large movements.
Blast Dragon Fist now uses an active melee trigger. The caster lunges through the target with the dragon visual, turns back, then fires a DBC/JRMCore ki blast visual.
Blast uses the DBC/JRMCore ki blast as a visual projectile shell while HexColorText keeps control of the real Dragon Fist damage through its own damage bridge.
Dragon Fist command testing remains available for normal, rush, loop, and blast while the future real skill version can later scale from Strength, Melee, skill level, and cooldown settings.
Optimization note: cinematic dragon rendering still scales by distance to help reduce unnecessary FPS cost during larger Dragon Fist visuals.
entries.actionSubtitle: Normal, Rush, Loop, and Blast variants were expanded.

entries.title: Dragon Fist testing expanded
details: Added the first Dragon Fist command test framework, including normal, rush, loop, and blast variants.
Added a Dragon Fist cooldown shelf HUD with a mini dragon icon, using the same style as the Black Coffin cooldown display.
Dragon Fist command cooldowns now support separate cooldown timers per method, so one variant should no longer lock out every other variant.
Improved Dragon Fist command autocomplete for the available variants and aliases.
Improved the Loop Dragon Fist variant with a longer cinematic loop, stronger return dive, and more burst hits when it reconnects with the target.
Fixed the Hex Hair custom-form color leak check so normal SSJ hair can turn yellow again while addon/custom forms can still use custom hair color when enabled.
Quieted extra JRM GUI debug output now that the action menu transparency debugging is no longer needed.
Added groundwork for future Dragon Fist activation through NBT skill gates, keybinds, scripting methods, and cooldown tuning. The /dragonfist command will remain as a fallback/debug command.

entries.title: Rarity Detection & EvolveReady Fixes
details: Fixed new-item EvolveReady tagging so it now uses the intended 18% chance instead of accidentally applying at a guaranteed 100% rate.
Restored auto EvolveReady eligibility to Common+ rarity items while still blocking unknown or non-rarity items from being tagged.
Improved Effervescent+ detection so it is less likely to be mistaken for regular Effervescent during rarity checks.
Fixed a JavaScript regex boundary issue that could make fallback rarity detection fail inconsistently.
Updated mod-side rarity detection so glints and overlays can read hidden rarity markers stored inside LorePages, not only visible lore.
Tightened Black rarity detection to reduce false Black glints from text such as Pseudo Black, Blackflame, or other flavor lines containing the word black.
entries.actionSubtitle: EvolveReady, Effervescent+, LorePages, and Black glint detection were cleaned up.

entries.title: HexColorText Progress Update
details: Fixed the rarity overlay bug so items without real visible rarity lore no longer incorrectly show rarity frames/overlays in the slot UI.
Added orb achievements and expanded the achievement path for orb progression and collection tracking.
HexTail production is halted for now aside from keeping the current solid tail color support; broader tail work is being put on hold until later.
Grapple work is still in active testing/debugging and DBC-specific grapple damage can be added in a later pass.

entries.title: Sort Button and Unsocketer UI Adjustments
details: The inventory Sort button was repositioned again for better placement on the player inventory and creative inventory screens.
Chest and container Sort button placement was left alone since those screens were already in a good spot.
The Hex Unsocketer GUI received another text alignment pass to better match the new template-based texture layout.
Open, Filled, and Max text were nudged for cleaner spacing, and the Inventory label was shifted slightly lower for readability.
This pass was focused on visual cleanup only, with no changes to unsocketing logic or sorting behavior.
entries.actionSubtitle: Small placement and text-spacing tweaks for inventory sorting and the Hex Unsocketer.

entries.title: <grad #F06A2A #FFB36B #F06A2A scroll=0.18 styles=l>Reroll / Rarity Cleanup</grad>
entries.summary: Cleaned up reroll rarity detection and older item presentation issues.
details: Adjusted reroll checks so items are less likely to fail when their rarity marker is not shown cleanly on the default visible lore.
Improved support for items whose rarity data or marker presentation lives on lore pages instead of only the first visible page.
This helps fix cases where evolved or older items could incorrectly say they had no valid rarity to reroll.
Continued cleanup around legacy rarity presentation issues where an old visible rarity line could still appear alongside a newer evolved rarity presentation.
Older items with messy visible rarity lines can still be refreshed with !rarity migrate if needed.

entries.title: Hex Utility and Optimization Pass
details: Inventory sorting was added with a smaller custom-shaped Sort button. It appears in player inventory, creative inventory, and many chest-style containers.
The sorter can merge compatible stacks while preserving NBT, rolled stats, socket data, and other custom item data.
Rarity overlays and held/equipped glints were optimized with a shared render cache to reduce repeated lore and NBT parsing.
Custom hair rendering received an FPS-focused cache and render-policy pass. Custom-form hair color support was also added as a configurable option.
The heavier custom-form overlay and renderer leak paths remain disabled by default for safety.
Black Coffin charging was tightened so locked users should no longer see the charge HUD or GUI when the skill is unavailable.
Socketing gained unsocket support. Players can remove a specific socketed gem or remove all socketed gems while preserving returned gem stats, effects, and NBT.
A dedicated Unsocketer GUI was added alongside the existing command and script API support.
entries.actionSubtitle: Use the new Sort button in inventory or chest screens. Shift-click support is included.

entries.title: <grad #FFD24A #FFF2A6 #FFB300 #FFD24A scroll=0.18 styles=l>Levelup Balance Update</grad>
details: Updated the levelup system for endgame rarity progression.
• Effervescent+ now supports the older Effervescent_ naming as the same rarity.
• Black can now be reached from Effervescent+, but the chance is intentionally extremely low.
• Black still has a pity fallback after many failed levelup attempts, so progress is never completely wasted.
• Random non-rarity items in the third levelup slot are now ignored instead of failing the levelup attempt.
• The third slot only contributes when it is a valid matching-type donor.
• Effervescent+ tier growth was adjusted: 2 levelup attempts count as 1 effective tier.
• Effervescent+ now gets small stat growth per levelup attempt, with a stronger bonus when a full tier step is completed.
• Other rarities keep their original levelup behavior.

entries.title: <grad #C38CFF #8DB7FF #C38CFF scroll=0.18 styles=l>Combine System Update</grad>
details: Adjusted combine behavior for <grad #FFFFFF #E8E8E8 #D0D0D0 #E8E8E8 #FFFFFF scroll=0.14>Pseudo Black</grad> and <grad #000000 #0A0A0A #141414 #0A0A0A #000000 scroll=0.14 styles=l>Black</grad> items.

• <grad #FFFFFF #E8E8E8 #D0D0D0 #E8E8E8 #FFFFFF scroll=0.14>Pseudo Black</grad> can now wildcard-combine with any rarity once, then continues using its normal combine cap with matching rarities only.•
<grad #000000 #0A0A0A #141414 #0A0A0A #000000 scroll=0.14 styles=l>Black</grad> can wildcard-combine with any rarity up to 2 times overall
then continues with <grad #000000 #0A0A0A #141414 #0A0A0A #000000 scroll=0.14 styles=l>Black</grad>-only combines while still respecting the normal cap.
• Combine now keeps the main rarity overlay properly instead of donor rarity lines leaking onto the result.
• Chaos tiers now keep only the highest rank present during combine instead of stacking multiple chaos lines.

• Accessory type lines are now deduped, and when accessory types differ the result keeps the best base type in this order: <grad #FFD7F7 #FFB6E8 #FFD7F7 scroll=0.16>Amulet</grad> > <grad #7FDBFF #A8ECFF #7FDBFF scroll=0.16>Ring</grad> > <grad #FFD24A #FFF2A6 #FFD24A scroll=0.16>Artifact</grad>.

entries.title: Hair / Black Coffin update/ Mod structure
entries.summary: Added more hair customization work and continued Black Coffin ability tuning, including GUI fixes, preview cleanup, and casting speed polish.
details: Continued work on the hair customization system, including GUI cleanup and picker fixes so color selection behaves more reliably without accidental extra button clicks.
More polish was added around hair preview behavior, split-color testing, and general customization flow for DBC-style hair editing.

Black Coffin received more tuning for its casting flow, with focus on making the build-up and coffin formation feel slightly faster and smoother without changing the overall feel of the move too drastically.
This update continues the ongoing push to improve both character customization and custom ability presentation as more edge cases are tested.

entries.title: Hex Renderer / Help update
entries.summary: Continued renderer and Hex Help tuning for outline behavior, nested style combos, and rabbit / easter styled text.
details: Continued cleanup and testing for the Hex text renderer, especially around outline behavior so outline styling does not get mixed up with nested gradients or outside wrapper colors.
Worked on combo support for newer style stacks including wave, rainbow, glitch, and rabbit / easter setups, along with continued testing for bold and nested style behavior.
Refreshed Hex Help examples and spring-style entries so newer wrappers and rabbit / easter examples are easier to test directly in chat.
More renderer polish and combo tuning is still ongoing as edge cases are found.

entries.title: Activity: Easter Event
entries.summary: Celebrate Easter with seasonal activities, festive fun, and limited-time rewards.
details: Join in on Easter-themed activities and enjoy special holiday fun during the event.
Look out for festive bonuses, seasonal rewards, and limited-time surprises.
More details will be available in-game through the event period.

entries.title: Renderer update: new styles, combos, help pages, and hub title support ✦
entries.summary: <grad #55FFFF #FFFFFF #AA55FF>The text renderer was expanded with new wrapper styles and better combo support, while hub feed titles can now use styled renderer text.</grad>
details: Added new wrapper styles including ChromaSplit, Afterimage, Scanline, Hologram, Posterize
Banded, Rune Reveal, and Rune alias support.
Improved combo behavior so newer wrappers work more cleanly with gradients, rainbow text, outline, shadow, glow, and other existing effects.
Expanded Hex Help with examples for the newer styles and added hub feed title support so entries can display styled titles directly in the GUI.
Nether portals can now be huge 64x64 max and 1x2 portal size(this means the portal itself not frame. So you would need at least 6 blocks.

entries.title: &CRaging &6Craft
entries.summary: Npc/script update
details: Updated rest of Dragon ball story npcs.
Fixed Orb roller bug with perfect core. As well the socketing issue imprinting to overlay.

entries.title: &cMod Hot Fixes
entries.summary: Quick Fix/release
details: This patch/upload was to fix the positioning of some text and get rid.
of some runoff text.

entries.title: Raging Craft
entries.summary: Npc/script update
details: Updated rest of tournament quest/npcs
Plans for custom story are underway, will need to rework existing custom story so far.
&cFinished Tournament npcs including Spopovitch (Majin Buu saga).

entries.title: HexHub live feed online
entries.summary: Remote GitHub feed is now working.
details: This is the first live entry.
Future updates can be appended here.

entries.title: New Command: Rarity Evolve Test
entries.summary: You can now test evolved rarity styling directly from the rarity command.
details: Use /rarity evotest 1, /rarity evotest 2, or /rarity evotest 3 on the held item.
Aliases /rarity evo <tier> and /rarity evolved <tier> work the same way.
The test applies your evolved rarity color formatting and writes the evolved tier and boost lines for previewing.
<#ff007e>Commands work in a single player environment and not on server unless permissions have been granted.</#>

entries.title: Planned: Useless backport
entries.summary: Planned backport of selected older mod features and blocks.
details: This is a planned backport of selected content from older mods.
The focus is on blocks, items, utility features, and older mechanics rather than bringing biomes over.
This notice is meant as a roadmap-style preview of older content that may return in a cleaned-up 1.7.10-friendly form.

entries.title: Git Hub Live Readings
entries.summary: Another GitHub Test
details: This was a live edit made about 9:22 PM, so it should show up and read from the GitHub.

entries.title: Server Update
entries.summary: Main Chat.js tweak/hotfix
details: Basically, some items in the auction would print as a pseudo black common but be pearlescent or something.
So that should be fixed persay, currently refreshing current story NPCs and planning custom story.

entries.title: New Feature: Tabbed News / Notes
entries.summary: The hub feed now supports local JSON and optional remote feed loading.
details: Edit config/hexcolorcodes/hub_feed.json to change these rows without rebuilding the mod.
If remoteUrl is set and reachable, the hub will try that feed first and fall back to the local entries.

entries.title: Maintenance: Hub shortcuts reorganized
entries.summary: Existing publish dates can stay fixed while new entries use TODAY.
details: Use a fixed date when you want a real publish date preserved on the row.
Use TODAY, LIVE, CURRENT, or a blank date field when you want the current day shown automatically.

entries.title: Activity: Join the Discord invite
entries.summary: Activities can still point at built-in actions like Discord.
details: This is a sample activity row using the built-in Discord action.
For a webhook-produced feed, point remoteUrl at a JSON endpoint rather than a Discord webhook URL directly.

entries.title: New Feature: Hex Help guide page
Notice: Item Info opens held-item data
Notice: Server Info opens getting started
